blocks:
Ayahs

إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦٓ أَنْ أَنذِرْ قَوْمَكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱)
Indeed We sent Noah to his people, [saying,] ‘Warn your people before a painful punishment overtakes them.’ (1)
قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّى لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۲)
He said, ‘O my people! Indeed, I am a manifest warner to you. (2)
أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱتَّقُوهُ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۳)
Worship Allah and be wary of Him, and obey me, (3)

Key Points

The story of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) is detailed in the Surahs Al-A'raf, Hud, Al-Mu'minun, Ash-Shu'ara, Al-Qamar, and Nuh. The most detailed account is in Surah Hud, from Ayah 25 to 49. The people of Noah accused him of manifest error. «انّا لنراك فى ضلال مبین»(1) They constantly mocked him. «كلمّا مرّ علیه قوم سخروا»(2) They called him light-headed and insane. «مجنون و ازدجر»(3) But the Prophet continued to guide them. The highest mission of the prophets is the call to Monotheism, «ان اعبدوا اللّه», obedience to the heavenly leader, «واطیعون», and observing piety (Taqwa). «واتّقو» Preaching must be step-by-step. Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) first warned his own people «الى قومه», but gradually conveyed his message to all people, and after their obstinacy, he cursed them. «لا تذر على الارض من الكافرین دیّارا»(4)

Characteristics of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) in the Quran: - Allah's special salutation upon him: «سلام على نوح فى العالمین»(5) Allah has sent salutations upon Abraham, Moses, Aaron, Elias, and His righteous servants, but the phrase «فى العالمین» (among the worlds) is not mentioned in any of those salutations. - He is the only Prophet whose period of prophethood is mentioned in the Quran (950 years). - He is the only Prophet whose wife, son, and people opposed him. - He is the only Prophet who lived the longest, built the Ark by Allah's command, the survival of animals was through his Ark, and he became the means of salvation for the human race, earning the title of the Second Father of Humanity (Abul Bashar Ath-Thani). - The first Prophet who possessed a universal book and law (Shari'ah).(6) - The Prophet through whose curse Allah drowned the earth in water. - The Prophet whose followers, after 950 years, did not even reach ten people.

1) Al-A'raf, 60. 2) Al-Anbiya, 36. 3) Al-Qamar, 9. 4) Nuh, 26. 5) As-Saffat, 79. 6) Kanz al-Ummal, Hadith 32391.

Messages

1- When recounting the history of past generations, one must suffice with the constructive parts; mentioning spatial and temporal details plays no role in moral development. «ارسلنا نوحاً»

2- For heedless individuals, warning is necessary, not glad tidings. «ان انذر قومك»

3- Punishment before clarification is not permissible. «انذر قومك من قبل ان یأتیهم عذاب الیم»

4- Religious warnings must be clear and compassionate. «انّى لكم نذیر مبین»

5- Following the prophets is the result and fruit of faith in Allah and the Hereafter. First Monotheism and piety (Taqwa), then obedience to the Prophet. «ان اعبدوا اللّه واتّقوه و اطیعون»

Ayah

يَغْفِرْ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرْكُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ إِنَّ أَجَلَ ٱللَّهِ إِذَا جَآءَ لَا يُؤَخَّرُ ۖ لَوْ كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (٤)
that He may forgive you some of your sins and respite you until a specified time. Indeed when Allah’s [appointed] time comes, it cannot be deferred, if you know.’ (4)

Key Points

Man possesses two appointed times (Ajal): an appointed time that is delayed by worship and piety, and hastened by sin and transgression. The final appointed time, which is unchangeable, is called Ajal Musamma (the determined term). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "People live longer due to their good deeds than they live according to their natural lifespans, and they die more often due to their sins than they die according to their appointed times." (7) Most people's lifespans are due to good deeds, and most deaths are due to sins; few are those whose life or death occurs naturally.

7) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 5, p. 140.

Messages

1- Past sins are forgiven through believing (having faith). «اَن اعبدوا اللّه... یغفر لكم من ذنوبكم»

2- Only a portion of sins, those related to Haqq Allah (the rights of Allah), are forgivable. «من ذنوبكم» (But Haqq an-Nas (the rights of people) must be forgiven by the person whose right was violated.)

3- Faith and righteous deeds lead to a longer life and the repulsion of calamities. «یؤخّركم الى اجلٍ»

4- The desire for life and enjoyment is a natural wish that has been made a reward for good deeds. «یؤخركم الى اجلٍ»

5- Life has a limit and an end, and it is unchangeable. «انّ اجل اللّه اذا جاء لایوخّر»

6- Fear the coming of death and believe. «اَن اعبدوا اللّه... انّ اجل اللّه اذا جاء لا یوخّر»

7- Man does not know the relationship between faith, righteous deeds, and longevity. «لو كنتم تعلمون»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى دَعَوْتُ قَوْمِى لَيْلًا وَنَهَارًا (٥)
He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have summoned my people night and day (5)
فَلَمْ يَزِدْهُمْ دُعَآءِىٓ إِلَّا فِرَارًا (٦)
but my summons only increases their evasion. (6)
وَإِنِّى كُلَّمَا دَعَوْتُهُمْ لِتَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ جَعَلُوٓا۟ أَصَـٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَٱسْتَغْشَوْا۟ ثِيَابَهُمْ وَأَصَرُّوا۟ وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ ٱسْتِكْبَارًا (۷)
Indeed whenever I have summoned them, so that You might forgive them, they would put their fingers into their ears and draw their cloaks over their heads, and they were persistent [in their unfaith], and disdainful in [their] arrogance. (7)
ثُمَّ إِنِّى دَعَوْتُهُمْ جِهَارًا (۸)
Again I summoned them aloud, (8)
ثُمَّ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَنتُ لَهُمْ وَأَسْرَرْتُ لَهُمْ إِسْرَارًا (۹)
and again appealed to them publicly and confided with them privately, (9)

Key Points

The interaction of Noah's people with him was such that they would sometimes take their children by the hand, bring them near Prophet Noah, and say: Just as our fathers advised us, we also tell you not to listen to this man. There are similarities between the disbelievers of Prophet Noah's time and the disbelievers of the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, including: 1. Fleeing from the truth The flight of Noah's people: «فلم یزدهم دعایى الاّ فرارا»(8) The flight of the Prophet's people: «ان یریدون الاّ فرارا» 2. Invitation to forgiveness Prophet Noah: «دعوتهم لتغفرلهم» The Prophet of Islam: «تعالوا یستغفر لكم رسول اللّه لوّوا رؤسهم»(9) 3. Arrogance and haughtiness Disbelievers of Noah's time: «واستكبروا استكبارا» Disbelievers of the Prophet's time: «ثمّ ادبر و استكبر»(10) 4. Refusing to listen to revelation Disbelievers of Noah's time would put their fingers in their ears so as not to hear. «جعلوا اصابعهم فى آذانهم» Disbelievers of the Prophet's time also said: Do not listen to this Qur'an and make noise during its recitation by the Prophet, so that perhaps you may overcome (him). «لا تسمعوا لهذا القرآن والغوا فیه لعلكم تغلبون»(11)

8) Al-Ahzab, 13. 9) Al-Munafiqun, 5. 10) Al-Muddaththir, 23. 11) Fussilat, 26.

Messages

1- Allah is aware of all human conditions, but supplication (munajat) is necessary. «ربّ انّى...»

2- Lamenting (or appealing) to the Divine Presence is the method of the prophets. «ربّ انّى دعوت قومى»

3- Guiding and propagating (the message to) the people must be continuous. «لیلاً و نهاراً»

4- If the ground for acceptance is absent, the Prophet's round-the-clock invitation will have no effect. «لیلاً و نهاراً فلم یزدهم دعاءى الاّ فرارا»

5- People are free and can insist on opposition despite the insistence of the prophets. «لم یزدهم دعائى الا فراراً»

6- Noah's invitation was always met with a negative reaction. «كلّما دعوتهم... جعلوا اصابعهم»

7- Obstinacy reaches a stage where the stubborn person neither listens: «جعلوا اصابعهم فى آذانهم», nor looks: «واستغشوا ثیابهم», nor gives up his action: «واصرّوا», nor yields: «واستكبروا استكبارا».

8- In propagation, the conditions of time, place, and the state of individuals must be considered. Sometimes it must be secret and private, sometimes public and general. «لیلاً و نهاراً... دعوتهم جهارا... اعلنت لهم... اسررت لهم»

9- The prophets did not fall short in their mission. «دعوت قومى لیلاً و نهاراً... دعوتهم جهاراً... اسررت لهم اسرارا»

Ayahs

فَقُلْتُ ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ غَفَّارًا (۱٠)
telling [them]: ‘‘Plead to your Lord for forgiveness. Indeed, He is all-forgiving. (10)
يُرْسِلِ ٱلسَّمَآءَ عَلَيْكُم مِّدْرَارًا (۱۱)
He will send for you abundant rains from the sky, (11)
وَيُمْدِدْكُم بِأَمْوَٰلٍ وَبَنِينَ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ أَنْهَـٰرًا (۱۲)
and aid you with wealth and sons, and provide you with gardens and provide you with streams. (12)

Key Points

Madraar (مِدرار) derives from darr (درّ), meaning the abundant pouring down of rain. In numerous verses of the Quran, the close connection between corrupt belief and action with drought and poverty, and likewise, sound belief and action with prosperity and security, is discussed. As He says in one place: «و لو انّ اهل القرى آمنوا واتّقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات من السماء»(12) If the people are pious and faithful, We will open the blessings of the heavens upon them. And in another place He says: «ظَهر الفساد فى البرّ والبحر بماكسبت ایدى الناس»(13) Problems on land and sea are due to the actions of the people. The goal of the Prophets is to save humanity from Divine wrath. From the beginning of the Surah until this point, repentance (Istighfar) has been mentioned three times. Ayah 3: «یغفرلكم», Ayah 7: «دعوتهم لتغفرلهم», and this Ayah: «استغفروا ربّكم».

12) Al-A'raf, 96.

Messages

1- Repentance (Istighfar) is from man, forgiveness is from Allah. «استغفروا ربّكم...انّه كان غفارا»

2- Pardon and forgiveness are among the Divine traditions (Sunan). «كان غفّارا»

3- To invite people to goodness, spiritual and material encouragement is necessary. «غفّارا... مدراراً»

4- The forgiveness of sins is the prerequisite for receiving subsequent favors. «استغفروا... یرسل السماء علیكم مدرارا»

5- Rain is one of the most important Divine blessings. «یرسل السماء علیكم مدرارا»

6- One of the effects of faith is the improvement of material life. «یرسل السماء... یمددكم باموال...»

7- Sometimes the abundance of wealth, children, gardens, and water is a Divine value and reward. «باموال و بنین و یجعل لكم جنات و... انهاراً»

Ayahs

مَّا لَكُمْ لَا تَرْجُونَ لِلَّهِ وَقَارًا (۱۳)
What is the matter with you that you do not look upon Allah with veneration, (13)
وَقَدْ خَلَقَكُمْ أَطْوَارًا (۱٤)
though He has created you in [various] stages? (14)
أَلَمْ تَرَوْا۟ كَيْفَ خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍ طِبَاقًا (۱٥)
Have you not seen how Allah has created the seven heavens in layers, (15)
وَجَعَلَ ٱلْقَمَرَ فِيهِنَّ نُورًا وَجَعَلَ ٱلشَّمْسَ سِرَاجًا (۱٦)
and has made therein the moon for a light and the sun for a lamp? (16)
وَٱللَّهُ أَنۢبَتَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ نَبَاتًا (۱۷)
Allah made you grow from the earth, with a [vegetable] growth. (17)
ثُمَّ يُعِيدُكُمْ فِيهَا وَيُخْرِجُكُمْ إِخْرَاجًا (۱۸)
Then He makes you return to it, and He will bring you forth [without fail]. (18)
وَٱللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ بِسَاطًا (۱۹)
Allah has made the earth a vast expanse for you (19)
لِّتَسْلُكُوا۟ مِنْهَا سُبُلًا فِجَاجًا (۲٠)
so that you may travel over its spacious ways.’’ ’ (20)

Key Points

The word waqār (وقار) means permanence (thubūt) and something that causes greatness (magnificence). Aṭwār (اَطوار) is the plural of ṭawr (طَور), meaning state, stage, or kind. Bisāṭ (بِساط) is derived from basṭ (بسط), meaning spread out, and fijāj (فِجاج) is the plural of fajj (فجّ), meaning wide road. The word rajāʾ (رجاء) is the opposite of despair (yaʾs). Whenever it is accompanied by a negative particle, it means fear (khawf). That is: What is wrong with you that you do not fear the greatness of Allah? Imam Baqir عليه السلام states: The meaning of the Ayah is: "لا تخافون للّه عظمةً" (You do not fear the greatness of Allah.) (14) Some have considered the intent of rajāʾ to be belief, because whoever hopes for something naturally believes in it. The meaning of the Ayah is thus: What is wrong with you that you do not attribute greatness to Allah? (15) Ṭibāq (طباق) either means agreeable and corresponding, or it means being placed layer upon layer. Man, in his emergence from the elements of nature and the earth, in his gradual growth, in becoming old and dry, in being fruitful or fruitless, in drawing sustenance from the earth, and in his constant need for a nurturer and gardener, is like other plants. "انبتكم من الارض نباتا" (And Allah has caused you to grow from the earth a [type of] growth.)

13) Rūm, 41. 14) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 15) Tafsir Rāhnamā.

Messages

  1. Warning in the form of a question is one of the methods of education. "ما لكم..." (What is wrong with you...)

  2. Attention to the stages of creation directs man toward the greatness of Allah. "لاترجون للّه وقارا و قد خلقكم اطوارا" (Why do you not attribute to Allah [due] grandeur, while He has created you in stages?)

  3. Noah عليه السلام, who lived thousands of years before Jesus and Moses, invited people to biology and cosmology. "خلقكم اطوارا... سبع سموات طباقا" (He created you in stages... seven heavens in layers.)

  4. Knowing nature is a path to knowing Allah. "الم تروا..." (Do you not see...)

  5. The entire existence is a class of Monotheism (Tawhid). "خلقكم, خلق اللّه سبع سموات, جعل القمر, جعل الشمس, انبتكم, یعیدكم, یخرجكم" (He created you, Allah created seven heavens, He made the moon, He made the sun, He caused you to grow, He will return you, He will bring you forth.)

  6. Divine actions are purposeful. "جعل لكم الارض بساطاً لتسلكوا" (He made for you the earth a spread [carpet], that you may travel.)

  7. Attention to the role of the sun and the moon in human life is a path to knowing Allah. "جعل القمر... نوراً و جعل الشمس سراجاً" (He made the moon... a light, and He made the sun a lamp.)

  8. Humanity needs travel, journeying, and wide roads. "لتسلكوا منها سبلاً فجاجا" (That you may travel therein wide roads.)

Ayahs

قَالَ نُوحٌ رَّبِّ إِنَّهُمْ عَصَوْنِى وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ مَن لَّمْ يَزِدْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَوَلَدُهُۥٓ إِلَّا خَسَارًا (۲۱)
Noah said, ‘My Lord! They have disobeyed me, following someone whose wealth and children only add to his loss, (21)
وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًا كُبَّارًا (۲۲)
and they have devised an outrageous plot. (22)
وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّا وَلَا سُوَاعًا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْرًا (۲۳)
They say, ‘‘Do not abandon your gods. Do not abandon Wadd, nor Suwa, nor Yaghuth, Ya‘uq and Nasr,’’ (23)

Key Points

In the Quran, the words aṣnām, awthān, nuṣub, and tamāthīl are used to mean idol, but the names of the famous idols of the polytheists are mentioned specifically. The names of five idols are mentioned in this Ayah, and the names of Lāt, ‘Uzzā, and Manāt are mentioned in Surah An-Najm.

Messages

1- Men of God bring their complaints to Him. «ربّ انّهم عصونى»

2- Relating instances of oppression serves as a lesson in steadfastness for others. «عصونى»

3- Whoever does not follow the rightful leader will follow the unworthy. «عصونى واتّبعوا من لم یزدهم... الاّ خسارا»

4- Noah’s lament is for the loss suffered by the people, not for his own isolation. «عصونى واتّبعوا من لم یزده ماله و ولده الاّ خسارا»

5- Not every wealth and child is a blessing. «لم یزده ماله و ولده الاّ خسارا»

6- Economic and social power causes people to be attracted, follow, and be influenced. «واتّبعوا من لم یزده ماله و ولده...»

7- The Prophets stand in direct opposition to the owners of gold, power, and deceit. «عصونى واتّبعوا من لم یزده ماله و ولده... و مكروا مكرا كبّارا»

8- The people of Noah neglected no evil deed: disobedience to Noah, following the unworthy, plotting and conspiracy, and insistence on preserving the idols. «عصونى... اتّبعوا... مكروا... قالوا...»

9- The doctrine of polytheism protects the interests of the owners of gold and power. «لا تذرن الهتكم»

10- For the admonition of others, sometimes we must mention the names of the vanished leaders. «وداً... سواعاً... یغوث و یعوق و نسرا»

11- The divine leader must recognize the peaks of corruption. «یغوث و یعوق و نسرا»

12- For the one who deviates from the straight path, numerous paths of deviation open up. «یغوث و یعوق و نسرا»

Ayahs

وَقَدْ أَضَلُّوا۟ كَثِيرًا ۖ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا ضَلَـٰلًا (۲٤)
and already they have led many astray. Do not increase the wrongdoers in anything but error.’ (24)
مِّمَّا خَطِيٓـَٔـٰتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا۟ فَأُدْخِلُوا۟ نَارًا فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا۟ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَنصَارًا (۲٥)
They were drowned because of their iniquities, then made to enter a Fire, and they did not find any helpers for themselves besides Allah. (25)

Key Points

The apparent meaning of the phrase "اغرقوا فادخلوا ناراً" (They were drowned, then made to enter a Fire) is that they entered the Fire immediately after being drowned. Of course, this is different from the Fire of Hell which they will enter after centuries; rather, it is the Fire of Barzakh (the intermediate realm). In the Quran, certain individuals and matters are introduced as means of deviation and misguidance, including: vain speech (lahw), spreading lies and rumors, misuse of sacred things, counterfeiting (badal namā'ī), Satan, the Taghut (tyrant/rebel), the deviant artist, uncommitted and religion-selling scholars who conceal the truth, those who prefer the world over the Hereafter, and the hypocrites. (16)

16) Luqman, 6; Al-Anfal, 144; An-Nisa, 44 and 60; Al-Munafiqun, 2; Al-Anfal, 36; Ta Ha, 79 and 85; Al-Ahzab, 10; Al-i Imran, 69; At-Tawbah, 9; Ibrahim, 3.

Messages

1- Anything that takes the place of the Truth leads to deviation and misguidance. «لا تذرنّ الهتكم... و قد ضلّوا...»

2- Negative propaganda has its effect. «قالوا لا تذرنّ الهتكم... و قد ضلّوا كثیرا»

3- Misguidance and deviation have degrees. «لا تزد الظّالمین الا ضلالاً»

4- Divine wrath and punishment are due to our own actions. «ممّا خطیئاتهم اغرقوا»

5- Polytheism (Shirk) is the foundation of errors. «لا تذرنّ الهتكم... ممّا خطیئاتهم اغرقوا»

6- The drowning of the people of Noah was the punishment for a portion of their deviations. «ممّا خطیئاتهم»

7- The Fire of Barzakh exists even in the midst of water. «اغرقوا فادخلوا ناراً»

8- The punishment for abandoning the Messenger of God is becoming helpless. «عصونى... لم یجدوا لهم من دون اللّه انصارا»

9- He who has Allah has not just one helper, but many helpers. «لم یجدوا من دون اللّه انصارا»

10- Against Divine wrath, wealth, idols, deceit, trickery, children, and supporters are all ineffective. «فلم یجدوا لهم من دون اللّه انصارا»

Ayahs

وَقَالَ نُوحٌ رَّبِّ لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ دَيَّارًا (۲٦)
And Noah said, ‘My Lord! ‘Do not leave on the earth any inhabitant from among the faithless. (26)
إِنَّكَ إِن تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا۟ عِبَادَكَ وَلَا يَلِدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا فَاجِرًا كَفَّارًا (۲۷)
If You leave them, they will lead astray Your servants, and will beget none except vicious ingrates. (27)

Key Points

It was revealed to Prophet Nuh عليه السلام that none of his people would believe except those who had already believed: «أُوحِيَ إِلَى نُوحٍ أَنَّهُ لَن يُؤْمِنَ مِن قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَن قَدْ آمَنَ» (17) Furthermore, the experience of 950 years of preaching was sufficient for him to recognize that this generation, and even the next generation, were beyond guidance, and thus He resorted to invoking a curse. Dayyār (دَیّار) is derived from dār (دار), meaning someone who resides in a house. Men of God do not think of themselves, but rather they think of guiding the generations of today and tomorrow. Although Prophet Nuh عليه السلام heard insults and endured torture, He stated the reason for His curse was their deviation and their act of misleading others.

17) Hud, 36.

Messages

1- Invoking a curse is permissible as a last resort. «رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْ... دَيَّارًا»

2- The mission of Nuh was universal. «عَلَى الْأَرْضِ»

3- Speak using the people's own language (or rhetoric). In response to [their saying] «لَا تَذَرُنَّ آلِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّا», Prophet Nuh says: «لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ...»

4- The intellectual health of society takes precedence over the physical health of the individual. It is better for those who mislead people to be destroyed. «لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ... يُضِلُّوا عِبَادَكَ»

5- Heredity plays an important role in upbringing. «وَلَا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فَاجِرًا كَفَّارًا»

6- Man reaches a point where he leaves no path for his own reform, neither in belief nor in action. Fājir (فَاجِرًا) refers to action, Kaffār (كَفَّارًا) refers to belief.

Ayah

رَّبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيْتِىَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًۢا (۲۸)
My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and the faithful men and women, and do not increase the wrongdoers in anything but ruin.’ (28)

Key Points

The scope of supplication (Du'a) must be broad. It should encompass both the past and the present, both oneself and one's relatives, and all believers throughout history. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The Mosque of Kufa was the house of Noah, and the meaning of "دخل بیتى مؤمنا" (who enters My house as a believer) refers to those who enter the Mosque of Kufa. In Karbala, Imam Husayn عليه السلام did not kill some of the opponents, saying: «انّ الذى لا اقتله ارى فى صلبه من اهل الایمان»(18) (Indeed, the one whom I do not kill, I see among his descendants people of faith). I will not kill any opponent in whose progeny I see a believer.

18) موسوعة كلمات الامام الحسین، ص ۵۰۵.

Messages

1- In supplication and curse, seek help from the blessed word "ربّ" (Lord). «ربّ لا تذر... ربّ اغفرلى»

2- Prophets, despite all their efforts, were not arrogant. A Prophet who said: «انّى دعوت قومى لیلاً و نهارا» (Indeed, I called my people night and day) says: «ربّ اغفرلى...» (My Lord, forgive me...).

3- In seeking forgiveness from Allah, prioritizing oneself over others is a commendable act. «اغفرلى و لوالدىّ...» (Forgive me and my parents...).

4- Relatives by marriage or lineage are included in divine favor only when they are also ideologically affiliated (believers). «و لمن دخل بیتى مؤمنا» (and whoever enters My house as a believer).

5- In supplication, ask for mercy and forgiveness for the believers, and for destruction and ruin for the oppressors. «ربّ اغفر... للمؤمنین... و لا تزد الظالمین الاّ تباراً» (My Lord, forgive... the believing men and women... and do not increase the wrongdoers except in ruin).
